author: doll-agent
content:
Research outcome: keep Element sealed; FerrayRecord covers the user-defined-type use cases.

Why Element is sealed:
1. Element invariants (dtype consistency, Send+Sync+'static, promotion rules) are too risky to let downstream crates impl arbitrarily.
2. The closed universe lets every DType match be exhaustive — opening it would force dynamic dispatch on dtype (slower) or break exhaustiveness.
3. Numpy NEP 42/43 user-dtypes work because Python is dynamically typed; Rust's monomorphization model can't replicate it without sacrificing zero-cost.

Three options analyzed:
1. Unseal Element with a contract: ~2-3 weeks across 4 crates, breaks DType::Copy invariant from #342, low payoff (most user-types end up reinventing FerrayRecord).
2. Opaque N-byte type: thinner version of option 1, leaves arithmetic unimplemented.
3. FerrayRecord: already covers practical user-dtype needs (physical units, fixed-point, etc.) at zero cost.

Decision: keep Element sealed. FerrayRecord + user-side op functions cover the practical need.

Reopening criteria: a credible Rust-native dynamic-dtype protocol emerges (ndarray standardization) or multiple crates request the same user-dtype kernel.

Closing as decided.
